heading: Credit for foreign taxes
label: 841 Credit for foreign taxes
notes: Amendments1986— substituted “section 801 or 831” for “section 801, 821, or 831” in introductory provisions, redesignated par. (3) as (2), and struck out former par. (2) which read as follows: “in the case of the tax imposed by section 821(a), the mutual insurance company taxable income (as defined in section 821(b)); and in the case of the tax imposed by section 821(c), the taxable investment income (as defined in section 822(a)), and”.Pub. L. 99–5141984— substituted “section 801” for “section 802”, wherever appearing, and “section 801(b)” for “section 802(b)”.Pub. L. 98–3691966— substituted “For purposes of the preceding sentence (and for purposes of applying section 906 with respect to a foreign corporation subject to tax under this subchapter), the term ‘taxable income’ as used in section 904” for “For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term ‘taxable income’ as used in section 904”.Pub. L. 89–8091962— added par. (2) and redesignated former par. (2) as (3).Pub. L. 87–8341959— struck out reference to in first sentence, and substituted “section 802, the life insurance company taxable income (as defined in section 802(b)), and” for “section 802 or 811, the net investment income (as defined in section 803(c))” in par. (1).Pub. L. 86–69section 811 of this title1956—Act , inserted references to section 811.Mar. 13, 1956

children.text: in the case of the tax imposed by section 801, the life insurance company taxable income (as defined in section 801(b)), and
in the case of the tax imposed by section 831, the taxable income (as defined in section 832(a)).
